scenario: Atlas Bank runs Camunda 8.8 SaaS for 21000 transactions per day. The operator has reviewed the migration preview for 320 active loanProcess instances moving from version 6 to version 7 and is now ready to perform the final confirmation step before any tokens are moved on the cluster by Operate.
question: Which final confirmation step does Operate require to start the migration operation?

options.text: Click Confirm to review the plan, type the word MIGRATE into the text box, and click Confirm again to start the migration operation on the affected instances
Click Apply Modification, accept the confirmation summary modal, and click Apply once more on the summary modal to start the migration operation on the cluster
Click Migrate in the toolbar, then click Continue in the appearing information modal, and click Apply Migration in the summary modal to start the migration operation
Click Next, then click Save Plan, and finally click Start Operation in the operations panel to start the migration operation on the affected process instances

a.text: Correct. The migration guide says the operator clicks Confirm to review the migration, types MIGRATE into the text box, and clicks Confirm again to start the migration operation on the selected instances.

b.text: Incorrect. Apply Modification and Apply on a summary modal is the commit pattern for batch modification, not migration; migration uses Confirm plus typing MIGRATE in the text box.

c.text: Incorrect. Migrate enters the migration view at the start of the flow, not the final commit; the documented final step is Confirm plus typing MIGRATE plus a second Confirm.

d.text: Incorrect. There is no Save Plan button or operations-panel Start Operation control in migration; the documented final step is Confirm, type MIGRATE, and Confirm again to start.

explanation: Per the migration guide, the operator clicks Confirm to review the migration, then types the word MIGRATE into the text box and clicks Confirm a second time to start the migration operation on the selected process instances.
